links.label: 栖息于
links.evidence: (2008) | range_map = Nothura minor map.svg }} The lesser nothura (Nothura minor) is a type of tinamou found in dry grassland habitats in tropical regions of east-central South America.Clements, J

links.label: 栖息于
links.evidence: The lesser nothura inhabits dry grassland habitats of tropical regions at an altitude range of . This tinamou can also be found in dry shrubland and savanna

links.label: 保护等级
links.evidence: status = VU

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: The IUCN has classified this species as vulnerable due to the fragmentation of its population and the loss of its primary habitat. It has an occurrence range of and a 2000 estimate of adult birds placed it at around 9000, which may be a little liberal. The habitats inhabited by lesser nothura are being rapidly destroyed by mechanised agriculture, intensive cattle-ranching, afforestation, invasive grasses, excessive use of pesticides and annual burning
